engineGuide.h2: Nissan Urvan Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Nissan Urvan across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: Introduced 1999 as Nissan's first common-rail diesel for Urvan, bringing improved economy but also turbo and timing chain concerns.
compatibleVariants: E24 Box Van, E24 Minibus, E25 Box Van, E25 Minibus, E25 15-Seater, E25 4x4, E25 High Roof
commonFailures: Turbocharger failure (VGT actuator sticking) 60k-90k miles
Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattle on cold start
EGR valve clogging leading to black smoke and power loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for YD25DDT Nissan Urvan engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: Updated 2005 with improved injectors and ECU mapping, offering better refinement but retaining core YD25 reliability considerations.
compatibleVariants: E25 Box Van, E25 Minibus, E25 15-Seater, E26 NV350, E26 Bus, E26 4x4, Export Models
commonFailures: Injector seal leaks causing diesel smell and hard starting
Turbo oil feed pipe degradation leading to oil leaks

entries.title: related issues on post-2010 Euro 5 variants
entries.cta: Get quotes for YD25DDTi Nissan Urvan engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: Smaller displacement option for lighter-duty Urvan applications, offering adequate power with simpler maintenance requirements.
compatibleVariants: E24 Box Van, E25 Box Van, E25 Standard Roof
commonFailures: EGR cooler clogging causing overheating and power loss
Glow plug relay failure affecting cold starts
Injector carbon buildup leading to rough idle

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Heavy-duty option for high-capacity NV350 variants, delivering strong torque but requiring careful maintenance to avoid piston issues.
compatibleVariants: E26 NV350 Box Van, E26 NV350 Bus, E26 15-Seater, E26 4x4
commonFailures: Piston cracking under high load (pre-2014 units)

entries.title: pressure fuel pump wear causing hard starting

entries.title: 2.7L Diesel
entries.history: Legendary naturally aspirated diesel known for simplicity and longevity, favoured by owners prioritising reliability over refinement.
compatibleVariants: E24 Box Van, E24 Minibus, E24 Export Models, Early E25 Imports
commonFailures: Head gasket failure on high-mileage units over 150k miles
Glow plug system failure affecting winter starting
Oil pump wear leading to low oil pressure warnings

entries.title: 2.7L Diesel
entries.history: Turbocharged evolution of the TD27, adding power for demanding commercial use while retaining mechanical simplicity.
compatibleVariants: E24 High Roof, E24 4x4, E24 Export Turbo Models
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing wear causing blue smoke and power loss
Intercooler pipe cracking leading to boost leaks
Injector pump calibration drift affecting fuel economy

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Modern petrol option introduced for ULEZ-conscious buyers, offering smoother operation but requiring timing chain attention.
compatibleVariants: E25 Box Van, E25 Minibus, E25 Export Petrol Models
commonFailures: Timing chain guide wear causing rattling noise on startup
VVT actuator failure leading to rough idle and check engine light
Oil consumption on high-mileage units over 120k miles

entries.title: 2.4L Petrol
entries.history: Robust SOHC petrol engine favoured for simplicity, though less economical than diesel options for high-mileage commercial use.
compatibleVariants: E24 Box Van, E24 Minibus, E24 Export Petrol Models
commonFailures: Oil sludge buildup from infrequent servicing causing bearing wear
Timing belt tensioner failure risking valve damage if not replaced
Exhaust manifold cracking leading to exhaust leaks and noise

commonProblems.h2: Nissan Urvan Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: YD25 Turbocharger Failure - Why Nissan Urvan 2.5L Diesel Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Urvan E24, E25, E26 (1999-2024, YD25DDT & YD25DDTi)
problems.rootCause: Variable geometry turbo actuator sticks due to carbon buildup from short-journey operation, causing boost loss and eventual turbo seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean VGT mechanism, replace actuator motor, reset ECU adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: May restore function if caught early; not viable if turbo internals damaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip turbo, replace bearings/seals, balance rotating assembly, test on bench
repairOptions.longevity: Good option if block is sound; 40k-60k mile expected life post-rebuild

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo, new gaskets, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Urvans; addresses root cause and prevents repeat failure

problems.recommendation: If your Urvan has under 90k miles and the block is healthy, a turbo rebuild from a diesel specialist can be cost-effective. For vehicles over 100k miles or with additional issues (timing chain noise, injector knock), a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo components offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt YD25 engine prices for your Nissan Urvan

problems.h4: Timing Chain Stretch - Why Nissan Urvan Diesel Engines Rattle on Cold Start
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Urvan E25, E26 (2005-2024, YD25DDTi & ZD30DDTi)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure over time, allowing chain to stretch and slap against guides, causing metallic rattling noise especially on cold startup.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, guides, tensioner and sprockets before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early with noise only; requires engine front cover removal

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if chain has jumped or caused valve/piston damage; eliminates repeat risk

problems.recommendation: If you hear a brief rattle on cold start that disappears after 2-3 seconds, book a preventative timing chain kit replacement immediately. If the rattle is constant or accompanied by loss of power, the chain may have jumped - at that point, a rebuilt engine is often more economical than attempting a complex in-situ repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Urvan timing chain replacement or rebuilt engine

problems.h4: EGR Cooler Clogging - Why Nissan Urvan Diesels Produce Black Smoke
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Urvan E24, E25 (2002-2010, YD22DDT & early YD25DDT)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation cooler becomes clogged with carbon deposits from short-journey driving, restricting flow and causing excessive soot production and overheating.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit EGR blanking plate, remap ECU to disable EGR function (where legal)
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective for off-road/commercial use; may affect MoT emissions test

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace clogged cooler with new or reconditioned unit, clean intake manifold
repairOptions.longevity: Restores emissions compliance; recommended for road-legal vehicles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR system or delete option, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if clogging has caused overheating damage to head or pistons

problems.recommendation: For Urvans used primarily off-road or in non-emissions-tested applications, EGR blanking offers reliable, low-maintenance operation. For road-legal vehicles, replace the cooler with a quality reconditioned unit and ensure regular highway runs to keep the system clean.
problems.cta: Compare Nissan Urvan EGR repair vs rebuilt engine costs

problems.h4: Injector Failure - Why Nissan Urvan ZD30 Engines Develop Rough Idle
problems.rootCause: High-pressure common rail injectors wear internally, causing poor spray pattern, fuel leakage into cylinders and uneven combustion leading to rough running.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one faulty injector, code to ECU, test combustion balance
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if only one injector failed and mileage under 100k

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors with reconditioned units, calibrate flow rates
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for vehicles over 100k miles to prevent repeat failures

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded injectors, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if injectors have caused piston damage or if vehicle has other high-mileage issues

problems.recommendation: If your NV350 has a rough idle but no smoke or power loss, start with injector testing and replace only the faulty unit. If multiple injectors are failing or you notice excessive smoke, replacing the full set prevents cascading failures. For high-mileage NV350s, a rebuilt engine with upgraded injectors offers the most reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for ZD30DDTi Nissan Urvan engine replacement

problems.h4: Overheating & Head Gasket Failure - Why Nissan Urvan TD27 Engines Fail
problems.rootCause: Age-related coolant hose degradation and water pump wear lead to overheating, causing head gasket failure and potential warping of the cylinder head.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace water pump, thermostat, hoses and radiator cap, flush system
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative measure; not viable if head gasket already failed

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, machine surface, fit new gasket and bolts, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if head is not warped and block is undamaged; 30k-50k mile expected life

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded cooling components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage TD27s or if overheating caused piston/cylinder damage

problems.recommendation: The TD27 is a simple, robust engine - if caught early, a cooling system overhaul can prevent head gasket failure. Once the gasket has failed, assess head warpage: if within tolerance, a gasket replacement by a diesel specialist is cost-effective. For Urvans over 150k miles, a rebuilt engine offers better value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt TD27 engine prices for your Nissan Urvan

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan Urvan
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with turbo failure on a YD25, timing chain rattle on a ZD30, or injector issues on an NV350, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Nissan Urvan specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. With nationwide delivery and supply & fit options available, you can get your Urvan back on the road without paying main dealer premiums.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan Urvan Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan Urvan and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

commonFailurePoints: glow plug relay failure affecting cold starts

items.title: Nissan Urvan 2.2L Diesel Engines

items.title: Nissan Urvan 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The backbone of the Urvan range, the YD25 common-rail diesel powered most E24, E25 and E26 variants, offering strong torque and reasonable economy but requiring attention to turbo and timing components.

items.title: Nissan Urvan 2.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The legendary TD27 naturally aspirated and turbocharged diesels powered early E24 Urvans, prized for mechanical simplicity and longevity despite lower power output than modern common-rail units.

turbo bearing wear on ETi variants

items.title: Nissan Urvan 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The heavy-duty ZD30 common-rail diesel powered late-model E26 NV350 variants, delivering strong torque for high-capacity buses and 4x4 models but requiring careful maintenance to avoid piston issues.

items.title: Nissan Urvan 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The QR20DE petrol engine offered a ULEZ-compliant alternative for urban Urvan operators, providing smoother operation than diesel but requiring timing chain attention on higher-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain guide wear causing cold-start rattle

items.title: Nissan Urvan 2.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The KA24DE SOHC petrol engine powered early E24 Urvans, offering robust simplicity and easy maintenance though with higher fuel consumption than diesel equivalents for commercial use.
commonFailurePoints: Oil sludge buildup from infrequent servicing

items.title: Nissan Urvan Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel power dominates the Urvan range across E24, E25 and E26 generations, with YD25 and ZD30 common-rail units offering strong torque for commercial use but requiring attention to turbo and timing components.
items.descriptor: Diesel power dominates the Urvan range across E24, E25 and E26 generations, with YD25 and ZD30 common-rail units offering strong torque for commercial use but requiring attention to turbo and timing components.
items.familiesLabel: Common Nissan Urvan Diesel Engine Families
families: YD25DDT/DDTi - most common replacement engine (E24/E25/E26), turbo actuator and timing chain risks
ZD30DDTi - heavy-duty NV350 unit, piston cracking on pre-2014 models, injector wear
TD27/ETi - legendary simple diesel (E24), head gasket and turbo bearing failures on high mileage
YD22DDT - mid-range option (E24/E25), EGR cooler clogging and injector carbon buildup
foundIn: E24 Generation (1995-2001) - TD27, TD27ETi, early YD25DDT
E25 Generation (2001-2010) - YD25DDT, YD25DDTi, YD22DDT, YD20DDTi
knownFor: Strong low-end torque ideal for loaded commercial operation
Turbocharger VGT actuator failures on YD25 units between 70k-110k miles
Timing chain tensioner wear causing cold-start rattle on post-2005 common-rail diesels
High UK replacement demand on E25 2.5L variants due to peak registration years

items.title: Nissan Urvan Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Urvans are less common in the UK but offer ULEZ compliance and simpler emissions systems, with QR20 and KA24 units requiring timing component attention on higher-mileage examples.
items.descriptor: Petrol Urvans are less common in the UK but offer ULEZ compliance and simpler emissions systems, with QR20 and KA24 units requiring timing component attention on higher-mileage examples.
items.familiesLabel: Common Nissan Urvan Petrol Engine Families
families: QR20DE - modern petrol (E25), timing chain guide wear and VVT actuator issues
KA24DE - robust SOHC unit (E24), oil sludge buildup if servicing neglected
VG30E - export-market V6, oil consumption and exhaust manifold cracking on age
foundIn: E25 Generation (2004-2012) - QR20DE, QR25DE (limited UK availability)
knownFor: Smoother, quieter operation compared to diesel equivalents
Timing chain guide wear on QR20 causing cold-start rattling after 80k miles
Lower replacement demand in UK due to diesel preference for commercial use
ULEZ compliance advantage for urban operators with petrol variants

engineYears.h2: Nissan Urvan Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Nissan Urvan's production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: E24 Launch: TD27 Naturally Aspirated Diesel
years.description: The first-generation E24 Urvan launched with the simple, robust TD27 naturally aspirated diesel, setting the reliability benchmark for early commercial variants.
keyChanges: E24 chassis platform introduced for UK commercial van market
TD27 2.7L indirect-injection diesel as standard powerplant
Box Van and Minibus body styles available from launch
mainEngines: TD27ETi - 2.7L turbo diesel (4x4 / High Roof export)
knownFor: Legendary TD27 simplicity and ease of repair
Head gasket failures on high-mileage units over 150k miles
Glow plug system reliability issues affecting winter starting
Strong parts availability through UK commercial vehicle breakers
years.cta: Get quotes for 1995-1999 Nissan Urvan replacement engines
years.ticker: Nissan Urvan TD27 engine replacement • Nissan Urvan head gasket repair cost • Nissan Urvan glow plug replacement UK

years.preview: E24 Facelift: YD25 Common Rail Arrives
years.description: The E24 facelift introduced Nissan's first common-rail diesel to the Urvan range, bringing improved economy and refinement but also new failure modes.
keyChanges: YD25DDT 2.5L common-rail diesel introduced alongside TD27
Updated front styling and interior trim for commercial appeal
EGR system added to meet Euro 3 emissions standards
popularModels: Nissan Urvan E24 Box Van 2.5L Diesel
knownFor: YD25 turbocharger VGT actuator failures between 70k-100k miles
Improved fuel economy versus TD27 but more complex diagnostics
Timing chain tensioner wear emerging as a common high-mileage issue
Strong demand for rebuilt YD25 engines from UK fleet operators

years.description: The all-new E25 chassis brought updated styling, improved cabin space and the refined YD25DDTi engine with better emissions and power delivery.

YD25DDTi introduced with updated injectors and ECU mapping
Optional 2.2L YD22 diesel for lighter-duty applications
popularModels: Nissan Urvan E25 Box Van 2.5L Diesel
knownFor: YD25DDTi injector seal leaks causing hard starting and diesel smell
Timing chain guide wear on post-2008 units causing cold-start rattle
EGR cooler clogging on short-journey commercial use leading to overheating
Peak UK registration years driving highest replacement enquiry volume

ZD30DDTi 3.0L diesel introduced for NV350 Bus and 4x4 variants

faq.h2: Nissan Urvan Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Nissan Urvan engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Nissan Urvan engine typically costs £900-£3,200 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. YD25 2.5L units average £1,200-£2,800, while heavy-duty ZD30 3.0L engines range £1,500-£3,200.
keyPoints: Prices vary by engine code, mileage and supplier location
Supply & fit adds £400-£800 to the engine price
Reconditioned units cost less than fully rebuilt engines
All EngineMarket quotes include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare Nissan Urvan engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine codes fit my Nissan Urvan?
items.answer: Common Nissan Urvan engine codes include YD25DDT, YD25DDTi, ZD30DDTi and TD27. Enter your registration above to verify the exact code fitted to your variant.
keyPoints: E24 models used TD27, TD27ETi and early YD25DDT
E25 variants predominantly feature YD25DDT or YD25DDTi
E26/NV350 models use YD25DDTi or heavy-duty ZD30DDTi
Petrol variants (rare in UK) used QR20DE or KA24DE
items.cta: Check your Nissan Urvan engine code compatibility now

items.question: Is a reconditioned Nissan Urvan engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes, when sourced from a reputable UK specialist. Reconditioned Urvan engines undergo cleaning, inspection and replacement of worn seals and gaskets before bench testing.
keyPoints: Reconditioned engines include new gaskets and oil pump
Bench testing verifies compression and leak-down performance
Minimum 12-month warranty covers parts and workmanship
Choose suppliers with Urvan-specific experience for best results
items.cta: Find trusted Nissan Urvan reconditioned engine suppliers

items.question: How long does Nissan Urvan engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch within 2-5 working days. Supply & fit installations take 1-3 days depending on variant accessibility and any additional repairs required.
keyPoints: E24/E25 variants generally quicker to access than E26 NV350
Turbo or timing chain upgrades may add 1 day to installation
Nationwide delivery means most UK locations served within 48 hours
Book supply & fit in advance to secure preferred garage slot
items.cta: Book Nissan Urvan engine supply & fit with UK specialists

items.question: What causes Nissan Urvan YD25 turbo failure?
items.answer: YD25 turbo failure is usually caused by VGT actuator sticking due to carbon buildup from short-journey operation, leading to boost loss and eventual turbo seizure.
keyPoints: Symptoms include loss of power, excessive smoke and whining noise
Failure typically occurs between 70,000-110,000 miles
Regular highway runs help keep VGT mechanism clean
Rebuilt engines include upgraded turbo components to prevent repeat failure
items.cta: Compare rebuilt YD25 engine prices with upgraded turbo

items.question: Can I replace my Nissan Urvan engine with a different type?
items.answer: Engine swaps are possible but require specialist assessment. Sticking with the original engine code ensures MoT compliance, emissions legality and straightforward insurance.
keyPoints: Diesel-to-petrol swaps require ECU, wiring and exhaust modifications
Upgrading from TD27 to YD25 needs mounting and loom changes
Any swap must be declared to insurer and may affect MoT emissions test
EngineMarket specialists can advise on compatible upgrade options
items.cta: Enquire about Nissan Urvan engine upgrade compatibility

items.question: Does Nissan Urvan engine replacement include warranty?
items.answer: Yes. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour at any UK garage.
keyPoints: Warranty terms vary slightly by supplier; confirm before ordering
Coverage includes consequential damage from engine failure
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
Supply & fit installations include workmanship warranty too
items.cta: Compare Nissan Urvan engine warranty terms from UK suppliers

items.question: How do I verify a used Nissan Urvan engine?
items.answer: Request compression test results, service history and mileage verification from the supplier. A reputable breaker will provide photos and a short test-run video.
keyPoints: Compression should be within 10% across all cylinders
Service history helps confirm maintenance intervals were followed
Mileage verification via V5C or service stamps adds confidence
Avoid engines with visible oil leaks or unusual noises on test run
items.cta: Find verified used Nissan Urvan engines with compression tests

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt and reconditioned Nissan Urvan engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines receive cleaning, inspection and replacement of worn seals/gaskets. Rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing known failure points like turbos and timing chains.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines cost more but offer longer expected life
Reconditioned suits budget replacements on otherwise healthy Urvans
Both include bench testing and minimum 12-month warranty
Choose rebuilt for high-mileage vehicles or known failure patterns
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs reconditioned Nissan Urvan engine options

items.question: Will a replacement Nissan Urvan engine pass MoT?
items.answer: Yes, provided the engine is correctly installed, emissions systems are functional and no diagnostic fault codes remain. EngineMarket suppliers ensure MoT-compliant installations.
keyPoints: DPF-equipped variants must retain functional DPF for MoT
EGR systems must be present and operational on applicable models
No warning lights or fault codes should be present at test
Keep installation invoices as evidence of compliant work
items.cta: Get MoT-compliant Nissan Urvan engine replacement quotes
